title: Padubidri Beach
tagline: Padubidri Beach — beach town of Karnataka

overview.short: Padubidri Beach is a coastal beach located at Padubidri in the Udupi district of Karnataka, India.
overview.description: Padubidri Beach is a coastal beach located at Padubidri in the Udupi district of Karnataka, India. It is one of the Blue Flag-certified beaches in the country, recognized for its cleanliness, environmental sustainability, and well-maintained facilities. The beach is known for its golden sands, tranquil waters, and lush green surroundings, attracting tourists and nature enthusiasts.

topPlaces.name: Padubidri
topPlaces.description: Padubidri /Padubidre (in Kannada) or Padubedre (in Tulu) is a town in the coastal district of Udupi in Karnataka state, India. It borders Mulki town in Dakshina Kannada District.

topPlaces.name: Yermal
topPlaces.description: Yermal is a settlement in Udupi district of Karnataka state in India, between Uchila and Padubidri on National Highway 66. There are two parts of Yermal, one is Tenka (meaning "south" in Tulu) and the other Bada or Badagu (meaning "north" in Tulu).

topPlaces.name: Adamaru
topPlaces.description: Adamaru or Adamaar or Admar is a village in the Udupi district of the state of Karnataka, India. The village houses one of the Ashta Mathas known as Adamaru Matha founded by Shri Madhvacharya, the Dvaita philosopher.

topPlaces.name: Nandicoor
topPlaces.description: Nandicoor (variously Nandikur or Nandikoor) is a village in Udupi district of Karnataka state in India. The Nandicoor village lies amidst paddy fields, coconut gardens and evergreen forests.

topPlaces.name: Udupi Power Plant
topPlaces.description: Udupi Power Plant is a coal based thermal power station in Karnataka, India, established in 2008. It is located to the north of Mangalore, west of Belmannu, and just to the northeast of Padubidri in the village of Yellur, Udupi district, close to the Shambhavi River, roughly 7 or 8 km (4.3 or 5.0 mi) from the coast from where the imported coal for the power plant is transported to plant through rail line.

topPlaces.name: Mulki, India
topPlaces.description: Mulki, also anglicized as Moolki, is a panchayat town located in Mulki taluk of Dakshina Kannada district in the Indian state of Karnataka. It is located on the Kanara coast, and also lies on the banks of the Shambhavi River and was originally known as Moolikapur, which later evolved into its present name.

topPlaces.name: Palimar
topPlaces.description: Palimar is a village and Grama Panchayat in the Udupi district of Karnataka state, India. Palimaru village is located on north banks of River Shambavi It houses Palimar Matha, one of the Ashta Mathas of Udupi founded by the Dvaita philosopher Madhvacharya.

topPlaces.name: Kemundel
topPlaces.description: Kemundel (Kemundelu) is a place in the Udupi district of the state of Karnataka, India. It is near to Yellur and Admar.

items.note: Padubidri /Padubidre (in Kannada) or Padubedre (in Tulu) is a town in the coastal district of Udupi in Karnataka state, India. It borders Mulki town in Dakshina Kannada District.

Yermal is a settlement in Udupi district of Karnataka state in India, between Uchila and Padubidri on National Highway 66. There are two parts of Yermal, one is Tenka (meaning "south" in Tulu) and the other Bada or Badagu (meaning "north" in Tulu).

Adamaru or Adamaar or Admar is a village in the Udupi district of the state of Karnataka, India. The village houses one of the Ashta Mathas known as Adamaru Matha founded by Shri Madhvacharya, the Dvaita philosopher.
Nandicoor (variously Nandikur or Nandikoor) is a village in Udupi district of Karnataka state in India. The Nandicoor village lies amidst paddy fields, coconut gardens and evergreen forests.

Udupi Power Plant is a coal based thermal power station in Karnataka, India, established in 2008. It is located to the north of Mangalore, west of Belmannu, and just to the northeast of Padubidri in the village of Yellur, Udupi district, close to the Shambhavi River, roughly 7 or 8 km (4.3 or 5.0 mi) from the coast from where the imported coal for the power plant is transported to plant through rail line.

Mulki, also anglicized as Moolki, is a panchayat town located in Mulki taluk of Dakshina Kannada district in the Indian state of Karnataka. It is located on the Kanara coast, and also lies on the banks of the Shambhavi River and was originally known as Moolikapur, which later evolved into its present name.

Palimar is a village and Grama Panchayat in the Udupi district of Karnataka state, India. Palimaru village is located on north banks of River Shambavi It houses Palimar Matha, one of the Ashta Mathas of Udupi founded by the Dvaita philosopher Madhvacharya.
Kemundel (Kemundelu) is a place in the Udupi district of the state of Karnataka, India. It is near to Yellur and Admar.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Padubidri Beach?
faq.a: The best time to visit Padubidri Beach is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Padubidri Beach?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Mangaluru International Airport (23 km) and the nearest railway station is Mangaluru Central (31 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Padubidri Beach?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Padubidri Beach at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Padubidri Beach?
faq.a: Stays in Padubidri Beach start from ₹1,200 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Padubidri Beach?
faq.a: The top attractions are Padubidri, Yermal, Adamaru, Nandicoor, Udupi Power Plant.
